era: Seattle, Washington. Garage/Space Rock. E=6 -- MAXIMUM ENERGY RETURNS. Fifth E=6 in engine history. First E=6 since degree 109. Quiet corridor (119-122) as decompression.

location.formative: Side project of Matt Cameron (Soundgarden, Pearl Jam) and John McBain (Monster Magnet). Formed 1994. The supergroup orbit -- maximum-energy musicians in unfiltered collaboration.
location.career: Wellwater Conspiracy -- garage/space rock. Three albums (1997-2001). Hollywood Records. E=6 -- the supergroup orbit.

members.name: Matt Cameron
members.note: Soundgarden (1986-2017), Pearl Jam (1998-present), Temple of the Dog (1991). The connective tissue of grunge. The hub of the supergroup network.

members.name: John McBain
members.note: Monster Magnet founding guitarist (1989-1992). Space rock pedigree. Heavy, distorted, feedback-drenched psychedelic guitar.

members.name: Ben Shepherd
members.note: Soundgarden bassist. Joined for the self-titled album (1999). Another Soundgarden connection deepening the supergroup web.

associated_musicians.name: Soundgarden
associated_musicians.note: Cameron was Soundgarden's drummer from 1986. His rhythmic power defined the band's heaviest material. Superunknown (1994) -- same year Wellwater Conspiracy formed.

associated_musicians.name: Pearl Jam
associated_musicians.note: Cameron joined Pearl Jam after Soundgarden disbanded. The only musician permanent in both Soundgarden and Pearl Jam.

associated_musicians.name: Temple of the Dog
associated_musicians.note: Cornell, Vedder, Ament, Gossard, McCready, Cameron. The grief supergroup. Cameron was in this supergroup too.

associated_musicians.name: Monster Magnet
associated_musicians.note: McBain was founding guitarist. Spine of God (1991). Crushing space rock/stoner metal.

associated_musicians.name: Mad Season
associated_musicians.note: Staley (AiC), McCready (PJ), Martin (Screaming Trees), Saunders (Walkabouts). Part of Seattle's supergroup economy.

theory_nodes.concept: The supergroup orbit -- acoustic element: the sonic result of maximum-energy musicians in unfiltered collaboration; garage rock immediacy plus space rock expansion; Cameron's unleashed drumming plus McBain's psychedelic guitar; E=6 as liberation energy
examples: Wellwater Conspiracy (degree 123) -- garage/space rock. E=6. Cameron (Soundgarden/PJ) + McBain (Monster Magnet) in unfiltered collaboration.
Contrast with main-band precision: Cameron's drumming in Wellwater is feral, unleashed. McBain's guitar is pure psychedelic environment.
Three albums document escalating ambition: Declaration of Conformity (1997, stripped two-piece) to Brotherhood of Electric (2001, fully realized).
theory_nodes.description: The supergroup orbit: E=6 energy from maximum-energy musicians in side-project liberation.

ancestors: The collapse frequency (degree 109, No-Anchor) -- previous E=6, unfiltered noise energy.
E=6 established at degree 36 (Hendrix). Five occurrences in 123 degrees.
genealogy.relationship: Fifth E=6. Acoustic element tracks at degree 124.

theory_nodes.concept: E=6 return after quiet corridor -- pattern: E=6 clusters (107-109 three-peat), rests (110-122 sub-maximum), returns (123); the quiet corridor as decompression chamber; measured ascent E=2 to E=4 to E=5 to E=5 to E=6
examples: E=6 gap pattern: 36, then 71 degrees to 107, then 1 to 108, 1 to 109, then 14 to 123.
E=6 is no longer once-per-hundred degrees. It clusters, rests, and returns.
theory_nodes.description: E=6 return pattern: cluster-rest-return. The quiet corridor as decompression between maximum-energy bursts.

ancestors: The maximum three-peat (degrees 107-109) -- three consecutive E=6, then rest.
The quiet corridor (119-122) -- four degrees of ascending sub-maximum energy.
genealogy.relationship: Fifth E=6 at degree 123. Track E=6 rhythm at degree 124.

theory_nodes.concept: The supergroup economy -- cultural pattern: Seattle's tradition of cross-band projects; Temple of the Dog (grief), Mad Season (addiction), Wellwater Conspiracy (liberation); Cameron as hub of the supergroup network
examples: Temple of the Dog (1991): Cornell, Vedder, Ament, Gossard, McCready, Cameron. The grief supergroup.
Mad Season (1994-1995): Staley, McCready, Martin, Saunders. The addiction supergroup.
Wellwater Conspiracy (1994-2001): Cameron, McBain, later Shepherd. The liberation supergroup.
Cameron in four cross-band configurations -- the most of any Seattle musician.
theory_nodes.description: Seattle's supergroup economy: cross-band projects driven by community interconnectedness. Cameron as the hub.

ancestors: Seattle's interconnected music scene documented across 123 degrees.
Cameron's main-band entries at various degrees (Soundgarden, Pearl Jam connections).
genealogy.relationship: Supergroup economy at degree 123. Track at 124.

cross_references.relationship: E=6 LINEAGE! First E=6 (degree 36, rock). Wellwater Conspiracy is the fifth E=6 (degree 123, garage/space rock). Maximum energy connects across 87 degrees and five different genres.
E=6 THREE-PEAT START. Degrees 107-109 ran three consecutive E=6. Degree 123 is the first E=6 since, after 14-degree rest. E=6 clusters, rests, returns.

PREVIOUS E=6. No-Anchor (noise/experimental, self-released, E=6) was the previous maximum. Wellwater Conspiracy (garage/space rock, Hollywood Records, E=6) is the next. Self-released vs. Disney-owned -- E=6 transcends distribution.
CAMERON CONNECTION. Pearl Jam at degree 83. Cameron drums for both Pearl Jam and the Wellwater Conspiracy. The drummer connects entries across 40 degrees.
CORRIDOR EXIT. Sabzi at E=5 was the last entry before E=6 returns. The quiet corridor (119 E=2, 120 E=4, 121 E=5, 122 E=5) as decompression chamber.
